note: Entity Block ships a single derivable block plugin (deriver) that renders any content entity in a chosen view mode as a block; no settings form, permissions, services, or submodules. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) since they probe documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site I want to render a specific existing node as a block, in a chosen view mode, without writing code. Which contrib module provides this and what does it give me?
evals.expected_output: The Entity Block module provides a block plugin that renders any content entity in a selected view mode as a block.

evals.prompt: How do I place an Entity Block to show a content entity? Walk through the steps.
evals.expected_output: Create the entity, then in Block layout or Layout Builder place the derivative for that entity type, pick the entity via the entity_autocomplete field and choose a view mode, then save.

evals.prompt: How does Entity Block make a separate block available for each entity type (node, media, etc.)? Name the mechanism and class.
evals.expected_output: The entity_block plugin uses a deriver, EntityBlockDeriver, which creates one derivative per content entity type that has a view builder.

evals.prompt: What does an Entity Block store in its block configuration, and where is the schema defined?
evals.expected_output: It stores the entity ID (`entity`) and `view_mode` (plus label_display); schema is block.settings.entity_block:* in config/schema/entity_block.schema.yml.

evals.prompt: If a user cannot view the entity referenced by an Entity Block, will they see the block? How is that enforced?
evals.expected_output: No — blockAccess() checks the entity's 'view' access and returns forbidden if the user cannot view it.

evals.prompt: By default an Entity Block does not show a title. How is its title generated, and how do I set my own?
evals.expected_output: With label_display off the title is auto-generated from the entity label (as '<entity label> (<admin label>)'); enable Display title to set a custom label.

evals.prompt: I want to reuse one piece of content across several pages/regions without duplicating it. Can Entity Block do this and how does it stay in sync?
evals.expected_output: Yes — place Entity Blocks referencing the same entity anywhere; the block merges the entity's cache tags/contexts/max-age so it updates when the entity changes.
